# Murmur

> A calm, private, local-first message & notes vault. Your notes are end-to-end
> encrypted in the browser and autosaved to a Google Sheet you own — no servers,
> no tracking, open source (Apache-2.0).

Murmur is a Slack-like web app for keeping personal messages, ideas, links, and
todos in channels. It is local-first (data cached as date-wise JSON in the
browser via IndexedDB), works offline, and installs as a PWA. A key derived from
your password (PBKDF2 → AES-GCM) never leaves your device; the login directory
only ever stores ciphertext, so it is zero-knowledge by design.

## Key facts
- Category: private notes app, encrypted note-taking, open-source Slack alternative
- Price: free and open source (Apache License 2.0)
- Data storage: the user's own Google Sheet (via a Google Apps Script they deploy) + local IndexedDB
- Privacy: end-to-end encrypted, zero-knowledge, no analytics, no cookies for tracking
- Platforms: any modern browser; installable PWA on desktop, iOS, Android
- Tech: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Google Apps Script

## Links
- App: https://murmur-chat.vercel.app/app
- Home: https://murmur-chat.vercel.app
- Source code: https://github.com/tusharmali/murmur
- Privacy policy: https://murmur-chat.vercel.app/privacy
- Security overview: https://murmur-chat.vercel.app/security
